commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR wires the existing MiniMax thinking wrapper into MiniMax simple-completion hooks, exports that wrapper through the provider-stream SDK surface, and adds MiniMax direct-completion hook coverage.

PR surface: Source +9, Tests +41. Total +50 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: the linked report gives the direct infer command and current main lacks a MiniMax wrapSimpleCompletionStreamFn on that path. I did not run a hosted MiniMax credentialed repro in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow repair can finish the SDK docs/API-baseline contract work without changing the MiniMax product direction.

target.title: fix(minimax): wrap simple completion requests
target.body:
## Summary

This fixes the MiniMax direct `infer model run` path so it uses the same MiniMax thinking payload wrapper as the embedded-agent stream path.

- Adds the MiniMax thinking wrapper to the provider simple-completion hook.
- Exposes the wrapper through the provider stream SDK barrel instead of importing plugin code through a core path.
- Adds coverage that `minimax-portal` simple completions get the M2.x `thinking: { type: "disabled" }` payload shim.

Out of scope: changing MiniMax M3 policy.

## Linked context

Closes #81607

Related #59824

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

No.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior addressed: `openclaw infer model run` was not applying the MiniMax provider wrapper on the direct simple-completion path, so MiniMax M2.7 responses with thinking blocks could be treated as no visible text.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on macOS, Node 24.13.0, using a temporary local Anthropic-compatible endpoint that returns the MiniMax-shaped `thinking` block followed by a `text` block.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH="$tmpdir/openclaw.json" OPENCLAW_HOME="$tmpdir/home" pnpm openclaw infer model run --model "minimax-portal/MiniMax-M2.7" --prompt "Reply with the single word: pong"`
- Evidence after fix: copied console output from the local CLI run:

```text
model.run via local
provider: minimax-portal
model: MiniMax-M2.7
outputs: 1
pong
```

The local endpoint log from the same run showed the real request payload shape:

```json
{"path":"/v1/messages","stream":true,"thinking":{"type":"disabled"}}
```

- Observed result after fix: the CLI completed successfully and printed `pong` from an SSE response where a `thinking` block came before the `text` block.
- What was not tested: a live request to the hosted MiniMax API.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: I do not have a MiniMax credential in this environment, so I used a local Anthropic-compatible endpoint to verify the actual OpenClaw CLI path, request payload, SSE parsing, and visible output handling.
- Before evidence: source inspection showed `MINIMAX_PROVIDER_HOOKS` had stream/replay/thinking hooks but no `wrapSimpleCompletionStreamFn`, while `prepareModelForSimpleCompletion` only applies provider wrappers through that hook.

## Tests and validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/minimax/index.test.ts src/llm/providers/stream-wrappers/minimax.test.ts src/agents/simple-completion-transport.test.ts`
- `pnpm lint --threads=8 extensions/minimax/index.test.ts extensions/minimax/provider-registration.ts src/plugin-sdk/provider-stream.ts`
- `node scripts/check-src-extension-import-boundary.mjs --json`
- `node scripts/check-sdk-package-extension-import-boundary.mjs --json`
- `git diff --check`

Regression coverage added in `extensions/minimax/index.test.ts` for the MiniMax simple-completion hook.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? `Yes`

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? `No`

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? `No`

What is the highest-risk area?

MiniMax request payload handling for direct simple completions.

How is that risk mitigated?

The PR reuses the existing MiniMax wrapper instead of adding a second policy path, and the wrapper already preserves the M3 thinking-enabled behavior.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Review and, if possible, run a live MiniMax `openclaw infer model run` check with hosted credentials.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

CI is running. Hosted MiniMax proof would still be useful, but the local CLI path now has real behavior proof.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

Initial CI lint findings were addressed in the pushed follow-up commit.
